system.specialQuality: iron hide, always hungry, easily provoked, afraid of fire
system.moves:
- Rip things apart: armor, weapons, flesh
- Eat almost anything
- Refuse to back off or let go, even when dying
- Recover from even mortal wounds, over time

When a boar lives long enough, and survives enough close calls, and dines year after year on the roots and nuts of ferro trees, it eventually becomes a raselbaedd. The size of a horse, with tusks like short spears and a hide that’s crisscrossed with scars and that bristles with broken blades. When you deal 2 damage or less to a rasel- baedd, before armor, your weapon chips or shatters on its hide. A raselbaedd’s hide (immobile, Value 2) can be harvested by a competent hunter with a fine steel blade (2 piercing, Value 2). It can then be fashioned into 1d4 raselbaedd cuirasses (2 armor, Value 3). Doing so requires: An exceptional tanner An upgraded workshop with fine steel tools (Value 3) A season of labor per cuirass
